resources.string: Wireless debugging pairing requires a WiFi connection.\nPlease connect to WiFi and try again.
Wireless pairing requires Android 11 or higher.\nYour device is running Android %1$d.
Open Settings → About Phone → Tap \"Build Number\" %1$d times\n\nYou\'ll see \"You are now a developer!\"
You may see a security warning — this is normal, tap OK to continue.
Open Developer Options → Enable \"Wireless Debugging\"\nThen tap \"Pair device with pairing code\"
Found! Tap \"Pair device with pairing code\"
In Developer Options, scroll down and tap \"Wireless debugging\" to enter
In Wireless Debugging settings, tap \"Pair device with pairing code\", then enter the %1$d-digit code in OpenCyvis

1. Tap "Open Wireless Debugging" below
2. Tap "Wireless debugging" to enter its settings
3. Tap "Pair device with pairing code"
4. Enter the %1$d-digit code shown in the dialog below
Tap \"Wireless debugging\" → \"Pair device with pairing code\", then enter the %1$d-digit code
Code expired. Please get a new one from Settings.
Could not find wireless debugging service.\nMake sure WiFi is on and Wireless Debugging is enabled.
Paired! Tap to return to OpenCyvis
Xiaomi devices may require a SIM card and Mi Account login to enable USB debugging security.
Please switch notification style to \"Android\" to use notification input.
Note: ColorOS may auto-disable Developer Options after restart. Re-pairing may be needed.
Please disable battery optimization to keep the background service running.
Tap \"Pair device with pairing code\" in the Wireless Debugging settings on the other side
Please open Settings → System → Developer options → Wireless debugging manually
